# Seldon

> External brain for your brain. See your ideas, generate new ones, find connections, find themes.

Seldon is a personal knowledge graph. You save notes — via Telegram from anywhere, or directly in the app — and Seldon builds a visual graph of how your ideas connect. It uses AI to automatically detect relationships between notes and surface hidden themes.

## What Seldon does

- Stores notes (called nodes) in a visual force-directed graph
- Each note supports an image attachment (WhatsApp-style: image at top, caption below) — shown both in the note card and as a thumbnail on the graph node
- Connects notes via weighted edges with a free-form text note describing the relationship
- Pulls notes from Telegram — text your bot a thought from anywhere, poll to sync
- Auto-generates edges between notes using tag matching, embeddings, or LLM scoring
- Traces paths through connected ideas (path tracing)
- Groups notes into workspaces — general (free-form) or time-aware (with a time axis)
- Generates narrative summaries of a note's connected subgraph (requires local Ollama)

## Telegram commands

- Send any text → saved as a note in the active workspace
- Prefix with `topic: ` → saved as a topic node (cluster anchor)
- Add `#hashtags` in text → extracted as tags on the note
- `switch to <name>` → switch to an existing workspace
- `switch workspace to <name>` → create a new general workspace and switch
- `switch workspace to timeaware <name>` → create a new time-aware workspace and switch

## API

Full OpenAPI spec available at: https://seldon.railway.app/docs

Key endpoints:
- GET  /graph-data             — full graph (nodes + edges) for a workspace
- GET  /graph-data/export      — download current workspace as a JSON file (nodes + edges)
- GET  /nodes                  — list nodes
- POST /nodes                  — create a node
- POST /nodes/{id}/image       — attach an image to a note (multipart/form-data)
- DELETE /nodes/{id}/image     — remove the image from a note
- GET  /nodes/{id}/neighbors   — direct neighbors of a node
- GET  /nodes/{id}/subgraph    — traversed subgraph from a root node
- GET  /edges                  — list edges
- POST /edges                  — create an edge manually
- GET  /workspaces             — list workspaces
- POST /workspaces             — create a workspace
- POST /telegram/poll          — poll Telegram for new messages
- GET  /link-proposals         — pending auto-generated edge proposals
- POST /graph/actions/generate-edges — run automatic edge generation

## Edge notes

Edges carry a free-form text note (stored as `edge.type`) describing the relationship,
e.g. "this challenged my earlier assumption". No fixed vocabulary — any string up to
300 characters. Weight is 0–1 (stored); the UI presents a 0–100 slider.

## Edge creation methods (Advanced)

- Tag Matcher — shared LLM-generated internal keywords
- Embedding Matcher — cosine similarity over sentence embeddings
- LLM Matcher — LLM scores each candidate pair directly
- LLM Debator — three-model debate (for/against/judge)
- Hub Matcher — clusters notes into emergent meaning hubs

## Tech stack

- Backend: FastAPI + SQLite + SQLAlchemy
- Frontend: Vanilla JS + SVG (no framework)
- AI: local Ollama (optional, for narratives and internal tag generation)
- Graph layout: custom force-directed algorithm
